creator: erwan@rail.eu.org
text:
At boot, unbound fails to start with the following error messages

Mar 26 22:31:01 nez-casse unbound: [653:0] notice: init module 0: validator
Mar 26 22:31:01 nez-casse unbound: [653:0] notice: init module 1: iterator
Mar 26 22:31:01 nez-casse unbound: [653:0] notice: openssl has no entropy, seeding with time and pid
Mar 26 22:31:01 nez-casse unbound: [653:0] error: Error for server-cert-file: /unbound_server.pem
Mar 26 22:31:01 nez-casse unbound: [653:0] error: Error in SSL_CTX use_certificate_file crypto error:0200100D:system
library:fopen:Permission denied
Mar 26 22:31:01 nez-casse unbound: [653:0] error: and additionally crypto error:20074002:BIO routines:FILE_CTRL:system lib
Mar 26 22:31:01 nez-casse unbound: [653:0] error: and additionally crypto error:140AD002:SSL
routines:SSL_CTX_use_certificate_file:system lib
Mar 26 22:31:01 nez-casse unbound: [653:0] error: util/alloc.c at 131 could not pthread_spin_destroy(&alloc->lock): Invalid
argument
Mar 26 22:31:01 nez-casse unbound: [653:0] fatal error: Could not initialize main thread

/unbound_server.pem is not mentionned in /usr/local/etc/unbound/unbound.conf

However after boot /usr/local/etc/rc.d/unbound start works without any
problem.

Except one must restart all daemons which relies on a working DNS
(apache, etc) at start time.

This beahvious was seen under 7.1-Release-p2 also

How-To-Repeat: Install unbound, reboot
